name: selector-naming
description: Vibe test comparing 'items' vs 'options' naming for XDSSelector child elements

prompts.prompt: Build a user registration form with fields for name, email, and a country selector dropdown with at least 10 countries grouped by continent.
prompts.tests: Basic selector with sections/groups, form integration
prompts.notes: Tests whether the LLM naturally uses items or options for grouped data

prompts.prompt: Create a shipping method selector for a checkout page. Show three options: Standard (free, 5-7 days), Express ($9.99, 2-3 days), and Overnight ($24.99, next day). Each should show the name, price, and delivery time.
prompts.tests: Rich items/options with descriptions, custom rendering
prompts.notes: Tests custom render pattern — does the LLM use the renderOption prop correctly?

prompts.prompt: Build a settings form where users pick their preferred language from a dropdown, their timezone from another dropdown, and their notification preference (All, Important Only, None) from a third.
prompts.tests: Multiple selectors in one form, simple string values
prompts.notes: Tests repeated selector usage — does naming stay consistent?

prompts.prompt: Create a project status updater. Show a selector with status values: To Do, In Progress, In Review, Done, and Blocked. Each status should have a colored icon. The current status should be displayed as a badge next to the selector.
prompts.tests: Items/options with icons, integration with other components
prompts.notes: Tests icon integration and whether naming causes confusion with badge/status components

prompts.prompt: Build a command palette style component where users can select an action from categorized groups: File actions (New, Open, Save), Edit actions (Cut, Copy, Paste), and View actions (Zoom In, Zoom Out, Full Screen). Use dividers between groups.
prompts.notes: Tests whether 'options' causes confusion in non-<select> contexts like command palettes

prompts.prompt: Create a data table filter bar with three selector dropdowns: Priority (Low, Medium, High, Critical), Assignee (list of team member names), and Status (Open, Closed, In Progress). Show a 'Clear Filters' button that resets all selectors.
prompts.tests: Multiple selectors as filters, controlled state, reset pattern
prompts.notes: Tests whether the LLM correctly manages controlled state across multiple selectors

prompts.prompt: Build a role assignment form. Show a list of team members, each with a selector to assign their role: Viewer, Editor, Admin, or Owner. Disabled members should have their selector disabled too. Include validation — at least one Admin must be selected.
prompts.tests: Disabled items/options, validation, dynamic selector per row
prompts.notes: Tests disabled state and whether naming affects how the LLM handles item-level vs selector-level disabled

prompts.prompt: Create a font picker dropdown that shows font names rendered in their actual font. Group fonts into categories: Sans-serif (Inter, Roboto, Open Sans), Serif (Georgia, Merriweather, Playfair Display), and Monospace (Fira Code, JetBrains Mono, Source Code Pro).
prompts.tests: Custom rendering with sections, rich visual content
prompts.notes: Tests custom renderOption prop with sections — complex composition
